NVT Name: PHP End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
Summary:
The PHP version on the remote host has reached the end of life
  (EOL) and should not be used anymore.
Solution:
Update the PHP version on the remote host to a still supported
  version.

Summary: PHP is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS:
PHP prior to version 5.5.37 and 5.6.x prior to 5.6.23 on
  Linux.

Summary: PHP is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS:
PHP versions before 5.6.40, 7.x before 7.1.26, 7.2.x before
  7.2.14 and 7.3.x before 7.3.1.

Summary: PHP is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS:
PHP prior to version 8.1.29, version 8.2.x through 8.2.19 and

Summary: PHP is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: PHP versions prior to 5.6.29 and 7.0.x prior to 7.0.14.

Summary: PHP is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Affected Software/OS: PHP prior to version 5.6.7 on Linux.

Summary: PHP is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Affected Software/OS:
PHP prior to version 7.4.33, version 8.0.x through 8.0.24 and
  8.1.x through 8.1.11.

Summary: PHP is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Solution:
No known solution was made available for at least one year
  since the disclosure of this vulnerability. Likely none will be provided anymore. General solution
  options are to upgrade to a newer release, disable respective features, remove the product or
  replace the product by another one.
